---
pagetitle: "EpiAware — composable tools for infectious disease modelling"
description: "A composable Julia ecosystem for infectious disease modelling."
toc: false
page-layout: custom
section-divs: false
hide-description: true
listing:
  - id: news-listing
    contents: news/posts
    max-items: 3
    sort: "date desc"
    fields: [date, title, description]
---

:::::: {.side-space}

<!-- Header -->
::: {.logo-header}

{{< include _includes/header.qmd >}}

:::

<!-- Approaches -->
::: {.approach-cards .section-start-space .section-end-space}

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-teal}
Compose distributions
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### Compose event trees
Wire delays into a tree — Sequential, Parallel, Resolve, Compete — one object that simulates and scores records with marginalisation.
[Explore &rarr;](tutorials/composing-distributions.qmd)
:::
:::

::: {.gallery-card}
::: {.gallery-banner .g-deep}
Assemble a model
:::
::: {.gallery-body}
### Assemble from parts
Latent process, infection mechanism, observation layers — each a swappable part through the single `as_turing_model` interface.
[Explore &rarr;](tutorials/assembled-model.qmd)
:::
:::

:::

<!-- Core Packages Section -->
:::: {.section-header .section-start-space}

::: {#core-packages-header-content}

### Core Packages
<p>The EpiAware ecosystem is built on a foundation of powerful, composable packages.</p>
:::

```{=html}
<a href="packages/index.qmd" class="button desktop-only-button">Explore Ecosystem &rarr;</a>
```
::::

::::: {.core-packages-grid .section-end-space}

::: {.package-card .box #core-card}
##### [ComposableTuringIDModels.jl <i class="bi bi-box-arrow-up-right" style="font-size: 0.4em;"></i>](packages/composableturingidmodels.qmd){.panel-title}
<p>Assemble infectious disease models from interchangeable parts as a single Turing model.</p>
:::
::: {.package-card .box #core-card}
##### [CensoredDistributions.jl <i class="bi bi-box-arrow-up-right" style="font-size: 0.4em;"></i>](packages/censoreddistributions.qmd){.panel-title}
<p>Handle the censoring and truncation biases common in epidemiological delay data.</p>
:::
::: {.package-card .box #core-card}
##### [ComposedDistributions.jl <i class="bi bi-box-arrow-up-right" style="font-size: 0.4em;"></i>](packages/composeddistributions.qmd){.panel-title}
<p>A grammar for composing distributions into event trees.</p>
:::
::: {.package-card .box #core-card}
##### [ModifiedDistributions.jl <i class="bi bi-box-arrow-up-right" style="font-size: 0.4em;"></i>](packages/modifieddistributions.qmd){.panel-title}
<p>Rescale, weight, or modify distributions, keeping the full distribution interface.</p>
:::
:::::

::: {.mobile-only-button-container}
```{=html}
<a href="packages/index.qmd" class="button btn">Explore Ecosystem &rarr;</a>
```
:::

<!-- News & Updates Section -->
:::: {.section-header .section-start-space}
::: {#news}
### News & Updates
<p>Read the latest from the EpiAware team.</p>
:::
```{=html}
<a href="news/index.qmd" class="button desktop-only-button">View more &rarr;</a>
```
::::
::: {#news-listing}
:::
::: {.mobile-only-button-container}
```{=html}
<a href="news/index.qmd" class="button">View more &rarr;</a>
```
:::

<!-- Built on Julia Section -->
::: {.section-header .section-start-space}
::: {#built-on-julia}
### Built on Julia
<p>We build in Julia for its type system, multiple dispatch, and automatic differentiation, on top of the scientific infrastructure.</p>
:::
:::
::: {.feature-grid .foundations .section-end-space}
::: {.feature-card}
### [SciML](https://sciml.ai)
Differential equations, sensitivity analysis, and scientific machine learning.
:::
::: {.feature-card}
### [Turing.jl](https://turinglang.org)
Probabilistic programming and Bayesian inference.
:::
::: {.feature-card}
### [Distributions.jl](https://github.com/JuliaStats/Distributions.jl)
The shared vocabulary of probability distributions our packages extend.
:::
:::
::: {.text-center .section-end-space}
New to Julia? Start with the [Using Julia](using-julia.qmd) guide.
:::

<!-- Background Section -->
::: {.section-header .section-start-space}
::: {#background}
### Background
<p>From R to Julia — the story behind EpiAware.</p>
:::
:::
::: {.section-end-space}
In R we built the [epinowcast](community.qmd) ecosystem and packages such as
[EpiNow2](https://github.com/epiforecasts/EpiNow2) and
[scoringutils](https://github.com/epiforecasts/scoringutils), now used by
academics and public health teams internationally. EpiAware is the Julia
equivalent: a domain-focused ecosystem in the mould of SciML or Turing.jl,
with community infrastructure like [rOpenSci](https://ropensci.org) and the
domain focus of [SpeedyWeather.jl](https://github.com/SpeedyWeather/SpeedyWeather.jl).

We are at an early stage and looking for collaborators —
[here is how to get involved](get-involved.qmd).
:::

::::::
